title: Batch orchestration versus stream processing
description: When scheduled DAGs over bounded intervals fit versus when continuous stream processing is the right tool.
body: Use when deciding between a scheduled pipeline and a streaming job. Batch orchestration runs bounded work per interval with clear reprocessing and backfill semantics; stream processing handles unbounded low-latency event flows with windows and watermarks. Do not force sub-minute latency needs into a cron DAG, nor run simple periodic batch as an always-on stream. Actions: choose batch orchestration when latency tolerance is minutes-to-hours and backfills matter, streaming when freshness must be near-real-time. Validation signals include latency met by the chosen model and backfills that reproduce history in the batch case. Risk boundary: a misfit choice bakes in cost or latency problems; if requirements straddle both, escalate the architecture decision rather than defaulting to the familiar tool.
